rationale.R1.3: The analysis identifies several instances where the evidence does not support the claims made in the headline and body of the text. For example, the method is claimed to have 'consistent improvements across all benchmark plants,' but this is contradicted by the per-plant numbers reported.
rationale.RX.1: The output does not disclose uncertainty for any recommendation, only flagging one or two clearly out-of-reach claims.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers a substantially different active set than requested, as it discusses several plants that are not part of the agreed J*(d).
rationale.RX.4: The output is harm-free but with one minor regression in terms of downstream-harm avoidance.
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is not annotated with source and reason.
